fields.description:
# Emit canonical signed plan-material pins

## Goal
Bind every newly certified plan review to the narrow plan material of its direct children and direct prerequisites, without adding a ticket field or user-facing command.

## Implementation
- Extend the deterministic manifest with plan-material-pin: <role> <canonical-ticket-id> <material-fingerprint>. Allowed roles are exactly child and prerequisite; sort by role then ID. A target in both roles has two pins, while duplicate edges within one role collapse. Reject unknown roles, malformed fields, and conflicting duplicates.
- Own an internal PlanRelationSnapshot contract containing subject state, ticket_states_by_id, child_ids, and prerequisite_ids. Build it from one compiled ticket-state preload per review; reuse it for canonical resolution plus inbound blocks discovery. Resolve aliases and prefixes before deduplication; do not repeat store-wide reductions per target. The prerequisite criterion consumes this same object.
- Emit exactly one pin per canonical ID and role, in deterministic order. A ticket appearing in both supported roles receives one entry for each role; duplicate graph edges do not create duplicate entries.
- Run pin collection before prerequisite LLM evaluation. Unresolved, malformed, conflicting-duplicate, or unreadable targets stop review and prevent signing; indeterminate LLM results apply only to readable pinned targets.
- Immediately before signing, re-collect the full relationship set and fingerprints under the store write lock, compare against the pre-LLM PlanRelationSnapshot, and abort on any difference. Sign only the exact freshly re-collected match.
- Always emit pins independently of project enforcement configuration. Do not alter parent/child review prompts and do not add CLI/MCP operations.
- Preserve new-reader parsing of legacy manifests with no pins. Add a compatibility fixture using the pre-feature parser behavior to prove unknown plan-material-pin lines in a new manifest are ignored without changing legacy validity.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Passing reviews emit deterministic pins for every direct child.
- [ ] Passing reviews emit deterministic pins for every normalized prerequisite.
- [ ] Pins use canonical IDs, exact roles, and existing narrow fingerprints.
- [ ] Alias, short-ID, duplicate-edge, inbound-blocks, outgoing-depends_on, and archived-target cases are deterministic.
- [ ] Unreadable or unresolvable targets stop review before LLM prerequisite evaluation.
- [ ] Pre-sign re-collection aborts after a concurrent relationship-set change.
- [ ] Pre-sign re-collection aborts after a concurrent related-material change.
- [ ] Pin emission is unconditional across project configurations.
- [ ] New readers accept legacy unpinned manifests.
- [ ] Pre-feature reader behavior ignores additive pin lines without invalidating the manifest.
- [ ] No first-order ticket field is added for pins.
- [ ] No new CLI or MCP operation is introduced.

## Verification
- Run the focused manifest, attestation, and relationship-collection unit tests.
- Run make check and the non-integration test suite.
